best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback continuation remains the core edge: 31 trades, $2415.30 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, no slow 12h trades.
best_edges.evidence: It is the top PnL profile and still has strong efficiency despite a -403.20 worst MAE. Median hold is 12.0 minutes and win rate is 100%, so risk work should narrow tails rather than suppress the whole profile.

best_edges.edge: Fast winners are the strongest portfolio-quality segment and should be protected from broad entry pruning.
best_edges.evidence: 57 fast winners generated $4884.80 with median MAE -29.75 and median hold 5.5 minutes. Many profitable fingerprints overlap weak-looking structure or veto reasons, so simple score/structure filters would damage the edge.

best_edges.edge: VALID_1DTE_ONLY breakout fallback is a real secondary edge, not just noise.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades, $1305.00 PnL, 168.76 PnL/exposure hour, no slow 12h trades. It has tail risk (-375.70 worst MAE, -123.86 avg MAE), but contributes 18.3% of total PnL.

best_edges.edge: Charm wall breakaway has high payoff per trade and good efficiency, but sample size is thin and MAE is heavy.
best_edges.evidence: 5 trades, $985.60 PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, median TP 12.0. Worst MAE -346.8 and avg MAE -148.96 argue for limited-risk tightening rather than expansion.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze long is moderate quality with contained worst tail versus other active profiles.
best_edges.evidence: 10 trades, $721.00 PnL, 187.16 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -208.25. It is less tail-heavy than charm_pin_pullback and pullback_long, so avoid global filters that clip squeeze winners.

broken_edges.evidence: 34 trades and $1237.70 PnL, but portfolio-worst MAE -527.00. Worst trade had ES-VWAP distance 26.20, negative VWAP acceleration, RSI 42.42, ADX 17.95, no early profit, and took 80.3 minutes to reach TP.
broken_edges.problem: Largest tail source despite positive PnL; the profile appears to mix clean fast pin pullbacks with extended/late failed-pullback entries.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributed only $162.80 across 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte in STALE_0DTE had avg MAE -210.63, worst MAE -250.25, median hold 52.6 minutes, and only 40.51 PnL/exposure hour.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback is low-efficiency and materially tail-heavy relative to its small profit contribution.

broken_edges.action: do not reactivate for this track
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long are configured but disabled or produced zero trades in this run.
broken_edges.problem: Dead profiles have no evidence for risk-reducer work. Activating them would be discovery, not risk reduction.

broken_edges.evidence: Setup-quality >=62 would remove the worst -527 MAE trade but retain only 45.0% of PnL. Hidden signatures that improve worst MAE often retain only 72-84% PnL and still leave large tails.
broken_edges.problem: Available broad thresholds remove too much profitable throughput for a risk-adjusted objective.

broken_edges.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long produced one trade for $91 with -11.7 MAE; breakout_long_no_0dte EXPIRED_0DTE produced one trade for $207.40. Both are interesting but under-sampled.
broken_edges.problem: Single-trade apparent efficiency is not robust enough to drive risk-reducer config decisions.

market_regime_read: The run is a short-sample, long-only, strong-upside regime with 100% realized win rate, no stop losses, no slow 12h holds, and low portfolio drawdown, but the path data shows the true risk: median MFE before MAE is only 0.05 points, TP before MAE occurs in just 0.9% of trades, and average MFE/absolute MAE is 0.84. Edge quality comes from eventual mean reversion/continuation after adverse movement, not clean immediate follow-through. Risk reduction should therefore target the extreme adverse-path subset, especially extended VWAP rejection misses and stale fallback participation, while preserving fast winners and high-PnL low-MAE winners.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate charm-pin pullbacks into clean fast-pin entries versus extended VWAP rejection recoveries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A single charm_pin_pullback_long profile currently contains both fast clean winners and the -527 MAE tail. A profile split may isolate tail-prone entries without broad global pruning.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Clean Pullback Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Existing charm pin, ES-VWAP distance, VWAP acceleration, pullback-from-high, setup quality, RSI/ADX state, and intratrade path labels for validation.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: When charm-pin pullback is valid but ES-VWAP extension and rejection state place the trade outside clean fast-winner fingerprints.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and average MAE by at least 25% while retaining at least 70% of its PnL and no slow 12h trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Create a stale/no-0DTE risk-off routing concept that sizes down or blocks stale fallback only in low-efficiency states.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: STALE_0DTE fallback has small PnL but poor exposure efficiency and meaningful MAE; a global no-0DTE disable may overreact to only 5 trades.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Stale 0DTE Defensive Fallback
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: option_data_state, profile, hold-time, MAE, MFE, setup quality, ES-VWAP distance, ADX/RSI, and stale/expired routing state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE data stale or expired with no clean high-momentum confirmation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Improve fallback PnL/exposure hour and reduce fallback avg MAE while preserving positive fallback PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Test dormant pullback_long_1dte as a config-only 1DTE alternative to breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Current 1DTE edge is profitable but tail-heavy; another existing 1DTE concept may capture fallback pullbacks with lower adverse path.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Fallback Discovery
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, option map state, ES pullback/reclaim, setup quality, ADX/RSI, MAE/MFE by profile.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY state where breakout_long_1dte currently fires into pullback-like rather than breakout-like structure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Any activation must beat breakout_long_1dte on average MAE and worst MAE while maintaining positive PnL/exposure hour.

next_experiments.config_bias: Surgical threshold nudges around the enabled extended-VWAP-rejection veto, especially thresholds that missed the six worst MAE rows by small boundary differences. Avoid broad setup-quality floors.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce the -527 charm-pin tail and second-order -400/-375 MAE trades with minimal PnL loss.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The enabled extended-VWAP-rejection veto is already near the correct tail fingerprint; small boundary relaxations should catch deep-MAE rows without suppressing protected fast winners as much as global filters.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Rejection Boundary Tightening
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE reduction with retained total PnL >=85% and retained fast-winner PnL >=90%.
next_experiments.risk: Some missed rows overlap protected profitable entries, including a fast winner with -346.8 MAE, so optimizer should test one boundary family at a time and reject broad overreach.

next_experiments.config_bias: Profile-specific charm_pin_pullback_long tightening around extreme ES-VWAP extension, weak/negative VWAP acceleration, weak RSI/ADX, and delayed profit behavior proxies available at entry.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long tail MAE while preserving its quick-profit winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin trade was not merely low setup quality; it was an extended VWAP pullback with negative acceleration, weak RSI/ADX, no early path strength, and long time-to-TP. A profile-local risk gate should outperform a global setup floor.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Guard
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and avg MAE, with profile PnL retention >=70%.
next_experiments.risk: charm_pin_pullback_long has 34 trades and many clean winners; blunt ES-VWAP max-distance tightening may remove the extended_vwap_winners fingerprint.

next_experiments.config_bias: Constrain STALE_0DTE no-0DTE fallback exposure first through profile-local selectivity or sizing, not VALID_0DTE profiles.
next_experiments.goal: Remove low-efficiency stale fallback tails with negligible impact on total PnL.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE contributes only $162.80, while breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades show high avg MAE and long holds; tighter fallback participation can improve risk-adjusted score with limited profit sacrifice.
next_experiments.name: Stale Fallback Risk Box
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback avg MAE and PnL/exposure hour, plus total retained PnL >=97%.
next_experiments.risk: Sample is only 5 stale trades; overfitting is likely, so validate across future runs before freezing.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test pullback_long risk filters only where ES structure/pullback state matches the hidden bad-MAE subset, not across all pullbacks.
next_experiments.goal: Lower pullback_long worst MAE from -403.2 without damaging the top PnL engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: pullback_long is the best edge but has bad-MAE rows tied to weak structure, low setup quality, and adverse early movement; profile-local structure quality tightening may help if it avoids blocking the 31-trade core edge.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Core Tail Trim
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long worst MAE, average MAE, and PnL retention >=85%.
next_experiments.risk: pullback_long supplies 33.9% of total PnL; any throughput loss can dominate the risk-adjusted gain.

next_experiments.config_bias: Optionally activate pullback_long_1dte alongside existing breakout_long_1dte in the one-dte router as a controlled discovery branch, then compare 1DTE profile attribution.
next_experiments.goal: Find a lower-MAE 1DTE fallback profile while preserving the current 1DTE profit contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current 1DTE breakout profile is profitable but tail-heavy; an existing pullback-oriented 1DTE concept may better match fallback state and reduce MAE.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Activation Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: 1DTE total PnL, average MAE, worst MAE, and PnL/exposure hour versus breakout_long_1dte baseline.
next_experiments.risk: This is discovery rather than pure risk reduction and may route trades away from a proven 20-trade edge.

research_notes: This is a single-run leaderboard with no experiment memory, so the plan should prioritize low-blast-radius risk controls over aggressive pruning. All realized trades were winners, which means PnL alone is misleading; intratrade MAE and exposure quality are the relevant risk lens. The strongest warning is that broad filters that catch bad MAE also block many protected good fingerprints. The best next optimizer behavior is sequential A/B testing: first surgical extended-VWAP-rejection boundary nudges, then charm-pin profile-local tail guard, then stale fallback containment. Do not optimize inactive active-router profiles on this track, and do not promote single-trade edges until more samples exist.
risk_controls: Require retained total PnL of at least 85% for any tail-MAE experiment unless worst MAE improves by more than 30%.
Require protected fast-winner PnL retention of at least 90% for any global entry-veto change.
Reject broad setup-quality or ES-structure floors unless they beat profile-local alternatives on retained PnL and MAE reduction.
Evaluate profile-local changes before global changes for charm_pin_pullback_long and pullback_long.
Treat STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE fallback changes as sample-limited; require confirmation in subsequent runs before freezing.
Do not expand disabled zero-trade profiles in this risk-reducer pass except the explicitly dormant pullback_long_1dte discovery probe if capacity allows.
Track worst MAE, average MAE, retained PnL, PnL/exposure hour, trade count, and slow 12h trades for every experiment.
